patch.value: Feature plan: add centralized status normalization utility, wire all status entry points, and preserve canonical in_progress persistence for backwards compatibility.
Canonical status alias normalization across CLI surfaces
Normalize status inputs so in-progress and in_progress are equivalent for command flags and imported payloads while persisting canonical values.

create/update/calendar/test-all and import/read surfaces accept in-progress and in_progress inputs equivalently; stored item status remains canonical in_progress; invalid statuses still fail deterministically.
All command and parsing status entry points are enumerated with baseline tests.
Status alias parity for automation clients
Removes avoidable failures caused by hyphen-vs-underscore status tokens
Improves robustness for existing users and agent tools
Status normalization is centralized and consistently applied
Users are reporting repeated failures from in-progress status usage
agent and user status updates stop failing on alias formatting

author: codex-agent
message: Start feature implementation for status alias normalization

patch.value:
Feature plan: add centralized status normalization utility, wire all status entry points, and preserve canonical in_progress persistence for backwards compatibility.

## Detailed implementation plan
1. Add a shared status normalization helper in core item utilities that:
   - Accepts canonical statuses and alias spellings (`in-progress` -> `in_progress`).
   - Normalizes case/whitespace and returns canonical enum values.
   - Preserves deterministic validation errors for unsupported statuses.
2. Wire the helper into every status input boundary:
   - `pm create --status`
   - `pm update --status`
   - `pm calendar --status`
   - `pm test-all --status`
   - importer status adapters (`beads`, `todos`)
   - item-document front-matter parsing/normalization for compatibility reads.
3. Keep persisted status canonical:
   - Storage and output remain `in_progress`.
   - Alias support is input-compatible only to avoid schema churn.
4. Extend regression coverage:
   - Unit tests for create/update/calendar/test-all status alias behavior.
   - Integration coverage validating CLI usage with `in-progress` and canonical readback.
5. Update documentation and release notes:
   - README, PRD, AGENTS usage notes, CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]`.
6. Verify end-to-end safety:
   - Build, targeted unit/integration runs, and full sandbox-safe regression run.
   - Record evidence on tasks/features/epic before closure.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Child tasks pm-ptal, pm-0kga, and pm-posc are closed. Alias normalization now accepts in-progress and in_progress across write/filter/import/read surfaces; docs updated and full sandbox test suite passed (61 files, 636 tests).
